title: region — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. Financial-adjacent events (tax_config, is_default) fan out to the audit + finance modules; lifecycle events (paused, archived) fan out to notifications + the Branch module (which caches the default region).

events.name: RegionCreated
events.trigger: RegionProvisioner completed successfully. Fires exactly once per (tenant_id, slug) pair.
consumers: branch::InvalidateRegionListCache (Wave 2b — Branch caches allowed regions for its dropdowns)

events.name: RegionUpdated
events.trigger: Any Region field mutated except status (RegionStatusChanged), is_default (RegionDefaultChanged), and tax_config (RegionTaxConfigUpdated). Fires for renames, timezone changes, locale changes, sort_order, weekend_days, subdivision_code.

events.name: RegionStatusChanged
events.trigger: Region.status transitioned. Umbrella event fired alongside the specific transition event (RegionPaused / RegionResumed / RegionArchived / RegionRestored). Downstream consumers subscribe to whichever level of specificity they need.

events.name: RegionPaused
events.trigger: Region.status transitioned active → paused. New bookings + new memberships referencing this Region are refused; existing rows survive unchanged.

events.name: RegionResumed
events.trigger: Region.status transitioned paused → active. Reverses RegionPaused. New bookings + memberships accepted again.

events.name: RegionArchived
events.trigger: Region.status transitioned to archived. Refused when is_default=true (observer catches it). Refused when any inbound row (Branch, Membership) still references this Region (REGION_IN_USE). Archived Regions vanish from selectors but existing rows stay valid.

events.name: RegionRestored
events.trigger: Archived Region transitioned back to active. Restoration allowed within the 730-day retention window (financial retention).

events.name: RegionDefaultChanged
events.trigger: The is_default flag flipped. Fires with the previous default's ID + the new default's ID. Audit-critical: the default region determines invoice calculations for regionless legacy rows.
consumers: finance::RecomputeDefaultAttributionForLegacyRows (Wave 4 — recomputes attribution for the small set of legacy rows that lack region_id)

payload.from_region_id: ULID | null (null when this is the tenant's first default — TenantDefaultRegionSeeded fires that path)

events.name: RegionTaxConfigUpdated
events.trigger: Region.tax_config JSONB mutated. Any sub-field change (default_rate_percent, rates, name, inclusive, registration_number, fiscal_year_start_month) fires this event. Financial-record retention 7 years.
consumers: finance::MaybeReplayFutureInvoices (Wave 4 — only when the caller carries `region_time_travel` entitlement)

events.name: RegionDeleted
events.trigger: Region soft-deleted OR hard-deleted. Distinct events not needed — the payload carries the mode.

payload.was_default: boolean (true means the tenant lost its default region — a system-invariant violation; the reconciler will alert)

events.name: TenantDefaultRegionSeeded
events.trigger: SeedDefaultRegionForTenantJob completed for a freshly-provisioned tenant. Fires exactly once per tenant lifetime (unless the tenant loses its default region + the reconciler re-seeds — that path fires a fresh event).
